concerns.text: Spec FR-001 and US-1 explicitly forbid generating synthetic data for inference when no single-source paired dataset exists, mandating a 'Data Insufficient Mode' termination. Tasks T015/T016 implement a 'Fallback Mode' that generates synthetic targets (decoupled/positive control) and proceeds with modeling, directly violating the spec's core data integrity constraint.

Constitution Principle VII and Spec FR-020 require evaluation on a *real* independent public dataset (e.g., Kaggle) for generalization. Task T045 substitutes this with a 'simulation' using generated data, failing to meet the constitutional requirement for generalization testing on distinct real-world data.

Spec FR-004 mandates nested hyperparameter tuning (inner loop for alpha, outer loop for evaluation). Task T026 conflates this with permutation testing logic (1000 permutations for outer loop, 100 for inner), failing to implement the required nested CV structure for Ridge regression.

Spec FR-007/FR-008 and US-4 require a prospective power analysis *before* data ingestion to gate the pipeline. The tasks place power analysis logic in Phase 4 (after ingestion/preprocessing) and lack a specific task to execute this gate before T013, risking processing of data that should have been rejected.

Spec FR-019 requires outputting the Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) test statistic and p-value to validate the null distribution. Task T033 defines the permutation p-value calculation but omits the explicit implementation and output of the KS test.

Spec US-4 requires calculating minimum N based on expected R²=0.1 before ingestion. Task T004 sets R2=0.0 as a default constant, creating a configuration mismatch with the required power analysis parameters.
